# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { Router } from "https://deno.land/x/oak/mod.ts";
import Kv from "https://deno.land/x/kv/mod.ts";

const kv = new Kv("edge_database"); 

const router = new Router();

router.get("/:userId/:filterParam", async (context) => {
  const { userId, filterParam } = context.params;
  const key = `users.${userId}.${filterParam}`;
  const value = await kv.get(key);
  
  if (value) {
    context.response.body = value;
    context.response.status = 200;
  } else {
    context.response.status = 404;
    context.response.body = 'Not Found';
  }
});

router.post('/:userId', async (context) => {
    const { userId } = context.params;
    const body = await context.request.body().value;
    
    for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(body)) {
       const kvKey = `users.${userId}.${key}`;
       await kv.put(kvKey, value);
    }
    
    context.response.status = 201;
    context.response.body = body;
});

await Deno.openKv("edge_database");

export { router };
